ideas.coreStatement: Test a neighborhood tool-sharing library with ten households to learn which tools people would share, while resolving responsibility for damaged or unreturned borrowed tools.

sourceSpans.quote: I keep thinking our neighborhood should have a small tool-sharing library so people do not each buy a drill or ladder they use twice a year. I want to test the idea with ten households on our block and learn which tools people would actually share. The blocker is figuring out who is responsible when a borrowed tool is damaged or never returned.

summary.text: Test a small neighborhood tool-sharing library with ten households.

purpose.text: Help neighbors avoid each buying infrequently used tools such as drills or ladders.

goals.text: Run a pilot with ten households on the block.
Learn which tools households would actually share.

statement.text: Responsibility for borrowed tools that are damaged or not returned is unresolved.

blockers.text: Determine who is responsible when a borrowed tool is damaged or never returned.

ideas.coreStatement: Create a single end-of-shift support handoff template containing customer, current status, owner, and next step, after consulting support leads.

sourceSpans.quote: At work, I want to replace our scattered support handoff messages with one end-of-shift template that lists the customer, current status, owner, and next step. I should ask the support leads what they need before drafting it.

ideas.coreStatement: Write a history paper comparing how two cities rebuilt public transit after major earthquakes, contingent on finding sufficient primary sources.

sourceSpans.quote: For school, my history paper could compare how two cities rebuilt public transit after major earthquakes. I need to confirm that enough primary sources exist before choosing the cities.

ideas.coreStatement: Create a shared family reunion signup page for meals, rides, and accessibility needs to replace separate text-thread coordination.

sourceSpans.quote: For the family reunion, I want a shared signup page for meals, rides, and accessibility needs so my aunt is not coordinating everything through separate text threads.

summary.text: Create one end-of-shift support handoff template after consulting support leads.

purpose.text: Replace scattered support handoff messages with a single template.

goals.text: Include the customer, current status, owner, and next step in the template.
Consult support leads before drafting the template.

questions.text: What information do support leads need in an end-of-shift handoff?

suggestedActions.text: Ask support leads for their handoff requirements before drafting the template.

summary.text: Write a history paper comparing how two cities rebuilt public transit after major earthquakes.

goals.text: Compare public-transit rebuilding in two cities after major earthquakes.
Choose cities with sufficient primary-source material.

blockers.text: The paper topic cannot be finalized until sufficient primary sources are confirmed.

suggestedActions.text: Confirm the availability of sufficient primary sources before choosing the cities.

assessment.text: Primary-source availability needs to be assessed before selecting the comparison cities.

post-earthquake transit rebuilding historical records primary sources

summary.text: Create a shared family reunion signup page for meals, rides, and accessibility needs.

ideas.coreStatement: Create a small native-plant pollinator garden with a rain barrel in the unused sunny corner beside the garage.

sourceSpans.quote: I would like to turn the unused sunny corner beside my garage into a small pollinator garden with native plants and a rain barrel.
I need to measure the space and check the local watering rules.

sourceSpans.quote: Separately, I want to start a monthly neighborhood garden newsletter that collects planting tips, photos, and seasonal reminders from anyone nearby, even people who never use my garden.

ideas.title: Native-plant pollinator garden with rain barrel

native pollinator plants for my area

summary.text: Start a monthly neighborhood garden newsletter featuring local planting tips, photos, and seasonal reminders.

purpose.text: Collect and share garden-related contributions from people nearby, including people who do not use the speaker's garden.

ideas.coreStatement: Create one pantry-first, phone-friendly meal-planning page that suggests three dinners and a short grocery list based on ingredients already in the pantry.

sourceSpans.quote: I want to make a meal-planning app that starts with what is already in the pantry. Actually, not a full app at first;
a simple phone-friendly page is enough. The important part is still the pantry-first meal planner. It should suggest three dinners and a short grocery list.
I keep saying app, but the idea is one pantry-based planner, not separate website and app projects.

summary.text: Create a phone-friendly pantry-first meal-planning page that suggests three dinners and a short grocery list from ingredients already on hand.

goals.text: Start meal planning with ingredients already in the pantry.
Suggest three dinners and a short grocery list.
Make the initial version a simple phone-friendly page rather than a full app.

ideas.coreStatement: Replace the hand-built Friday client reporting spreadsheet with a dashboard that refreshes from the warehouse so account managers have the same numbers before customer calls; the project requires security review and is currently blocked by lack of budget for a connector license.

sourceSpans.quote: For the client reporting project at work, I want to replace the hand-built Friday spreadsheet with a dashboard that refreshes from the warehouse. The purpose is to give account managers the same numbers before their customer calls. Budget is the blocker because this quarter has no allocation for the connector license. Security review is required too, but the missing budget is what currently stops the project.

ideas.title: Automate client reporting with a warehouse-backed dashboard

summary.text: Replace the hand-built Friday client-reporting spreadsheet with a dashboard that refreshes from the warehouse.

purpose.text: Give account managers the same numbers before customer calls.

ideas.coreStatement: Reorganize the garage workshop so repairs can start without moving boxes or searching for tools, using a clear bench, labeled common-tool drawers, and grouped bicycle supplies.

sourceSpans.quote: The garage workshop is frustrating because every small repair starts with moving boxes and searching for tools. I picture one clear bench, labeled drawers for the common tools, and the bicycle supplies hanging together on the wall. I could begin by measuring the bench and sorting one shelf this Saturday.

summary.text: Reorganize the garage workshop to make small repairs easier to start.

purpose.text: Enable repairs without moving boxes or searching for tools.

goals.text: Create one clear workbench.
Use labeled drawers for common tools.
Group bicycle supplies together on the wall.

statement.text: Small repairs are frustrating because they require moving boxes and searching for tools.

suggestedActions.text: Measure the bench and sort one shelf this Saturday.

ideas.coreStatement: Research nearby and online evening data science degree or certificate programs that can be completed while working full time, comparing cost, schedule, math prerequisites, completion time, and employer recognition before creating a shortlist.

sourceSpans.quote: I am considering an evening data science degree or certificate while I keep working full time. I need to compare nearby and online programs on total cost, weekly schedule, prerequisite math courses, completion time, and whether employers recognize the credential. I do not know which schools belong on the shortlist yet, so the next useful step is research rather than choosing a program.

summary.text: Compare nearby and online evening data science degree or certificate programs that can be completed while working full time.

purpose.text: Create a shortlist of suitable programs before choosing one.

goals.text: Compare total cost, weekly schedule, math prerequisites, completion time, and employer recognition.
Find programs compatible with full-time work.

statement.text: No schools have yet been identified for the shortlist.

questions.text: Which nearby or online programs should be included in the shortlist?

suggestedActions.text: Research eligible nearby and online programs before selecting a program.
Build a comparison table using cost, schedule, prerequisites, completion time, and employer recognition.

assessment.text: Research is the stated next step because the shortlist has not yet been determined.

first I need to measure that wall and check the leftover lumber in the garage.
That is the one project; the repeated words are just me thinking out loud.

ideas.coreStatement: Create and print a family birthday recipe book featuring one favorite recipe and a short story from each relative before the grandmother's birthday; recipes and photos are still needed from everyone.

sourceSpans.quote: My actual idea is to make a family birthday recipe book with one favorite recipe and a short story from each relative. I want to print a few copies before my grandmother's birthday, but I still need everyone to send their recipe and photo.

make a spreadsheet with the item, quantity, and expiration date.
create the pantry spreadsheet before thinking about barcode scanning or reminders.
